entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now

dmitry_yudin
Participant
 
Saturday
0 Kudos
 

Hi @leon3 , hi @Kushal_Banerjee , 

the password for hana is the same as for developer user

============================

Hello Dmitry Yudin,

Here I am NOT asking about the Abap Users which I know it was given in the Docker website itself,  you can see in the Docker --> overview section.

I am asking about : To connect HANA DB from Hana Studio there I need SYSTEM DB User_id password. I am asking about this password.

Rest I have managed everything of my own.

Thanks & Regards

Kushal

entry.title: Change report painter period parameter transferred to RKPEP000
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing a problem about report painter and called up report RKPEP000, if you have any idea about this case , could you please advise? Thanks.

The report painter report is created in group 6P3 which is related to WBS element. When I executed the report, it shows the first screen and then I double click the line at the screen and the second screen displays(The second screen is the called up report RKPEP000).

At the first screen, a column is "balance by the first-half of current year", it shows the data of below two parts:

For example, current year is 2024, the first part is "period 1~12, year 1900~2023" and the second part is "period 1~6, year 2024". There is no problem at the first screen, but at the second screen, the report shows data of "period 1~12, year 1900~2024", the period 7~12 year 2024's data should not be showed.

The reason is that the parameter transferred to the second screen is combined, so the period is 1~12.

Is there any method can solve this problem? I want to show the correct data at the second screen.

Can anybody from the component\app owner team in SAP provide a solution for this? Thanks much.

entry.title: Organizational Unit error for business partner
entry.content:
Hi all, I am using the SAP Practice for Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Transportation Management. I hit a problem with creating (saving) a freight agreement in exercise 4. The error is:

Organizational Unit 1010: No data maintained for business partner CAR-020

I have created the carrier and it is indeed in the system so I am not sure why I am getting this issue. Is this the right channel for me to report issue?

Cheers

SFC

entry.title: Service Confirmation is cancelled but the status remains Not Started
entry.content:
The service confirmation lines are all cancelled, but the service confirmation status remains Not Started instead of Cancelled.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Service Confirmation is cancelled but the status rema...
entry.content:
The service confirmation is cancelled before released, so the document and item status remain Not Started, even though the document is cancelled.

In the service confirmations view, open the advanced search. Set the cancellation status=cancelled and search the confirmation, you can see the confirmation is filtered out, this means that the confirmation is cancelled. There are several status on a service confirmation: status; cancellation status; approval status; delivery status and invoice status. 

To confirm the cancellation status, you will need to check the cancellation status.

This is the correct system designed behavior.

entry.title: Re: Cannot able to combine the individual production orde...
entry.content: Hi， I also encountered the same problem, and later found that the header unit of the process route for obtaining production orders was different from the basic unit of measurement.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements ListReport with OData V4 - Cannot hide...
entry.content:
Hi,

This is by design and can be seen in the note in https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/c0f6592a592e47f9bb6d09900de47412:

"If the path-based value for UI.Hidden evaluates to true for all rows, then only the fields are hidden and not the entire column."

The reasoning is that if the entire column were to be hidden, it causes confusion to end user when dealing with variants. For example, if currently all the rows have no values and the column is hidden and if user saves this as a variant - what should happen when this variant is restored in a context where some of the rows have a value for this column? Suddenly bringing back the column might confuse the user who saw this variant w/o the column earlier!

Best Regards,

   Sandeep

entry.title: Re: Use group separator for barcode in Adobe Forms
entry.content: Can you explain how you did that by using Smartforms?

entry.title: Monitor/Manage applications in S4 HANA disappear intermittently
entry.content:
We have intermittent issues when apps intermittently disappear from the launchpad. 

No business role assignments were changed and clearing the cache sometimes works or waiting till the next day "fixes" the issue.

entry.title: How to restrict authorization for accounting document reversal (FB08) and reset cleared item (FBRA)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are writing to request detailed guidance on how to control authorization for accounting document reversal (FB08) and reset cleared item (FBRA) in our SAP Public Cloud environment.

Currently, we have to restrict these authorizations to only 1-2 members of our Customer accounting team. In SAP on-premise version, we controlled these authorizations through T-code (FB08, FBRA) access. However, we have not been able to locate the corresponding apps in the Public Cloud environment.

 

We would appreciate it if you could provide us with detailed instructions on how to achieve this restriction in the Public Clou

Hana7

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR Create Putaway Warehouse Task for...
entry.content:
after some trial-error , 

this code below is worked for my case

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-rdoccat "(Inbound Delivery Doc Cat)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-rdocid  "(Inbound Delivery Doc ID)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-ritmid  "(Inbound Delivery Item ID)

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlpla   "(Target S.Bin)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nltyp   "(Target S.Type)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlber  ​ "(Target Storage Section)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-nlner  ​ "(Target HU)

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-vlner  ​ "(Source HU)

<ls_create_whr_putaway>-anfme   "(Quantity of HU)
<ls_create_whr_putaway>-altme   "(Quantity UOM)

CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/TO_CREATE_WHR'
  EXPORTING
    iv_lgnum       = '0100'
    iv_process     = wmegc_whr_proc_pu
    iv_mve_hu      = 'X'
    iv_to_init     = 'X'
    iv_bname       = sy-uname
    it_create_whr  = lt_create_putaway_single
    iv_update_task = ' '
    iv_commit_work = abap_true
  IMPORTING
    ev_tanum       = lv_wt_result
    et_ltap_vb     = lt_ltap_vb
    et_bapiret     = lt_bapiret
    ev_severity    = lv_severity.

entry.title: Re: How to Reduce Import Time for 1M Records in SAP Custo...
entry.content:
Hi @Rvanjari 

I'm not sure from which systems and by which methods like API or file you're importing the user accounts to CDC, could you also check the other system and make sure the data extraction and transmission performance of the source system is good.
On the CDC side, you may try splitting the 1M user records to multiple batches and running multiple dataflows in parallel, it might improve the performance.

entry.title: Re: Use of elements not permitted in CDS view Data Definition. HANA on-prem.
entry.content: I'm having the same problem now, Could I know how did you solve it?

entry.title: Re: Organizational Unit error for business partner
entry.content:
It sounds like you're facing an issue with a freight agreement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, particularly with the business partner CAR-020 not being recognized, even though you've created it in the system. This error generally points to a configuration or data issue, where the business partner might not be correctly linked to the relevant organizational unit, or there might be missing master data for the carrier in the Transportation Management (TM) configuration.

As for whether this is the right channel for reporting issues: this platform isn't specifically designed for reporting SAP issues directly to SAP support, but I can still help troubleshoot and guide you through potential solutions! Here are some things you can check:

Steps to troubleshoot:

  1. Check Business Partner Role Assignment:

    • Go to the business partner master data for "CAR-020" and ensure that it has the correct roles assigned for use in Transportation Management.
    • Specifically, make sure the role "Carrier" is assigned to it.

  2. Verify Organizational Unit Configuration:

    • The organizational unit "1010" could be referencing a location or a business unit where the carrier data should be connected. Ensure the carrier "CAR-020" is properly maintained for this specific organizational unit.
    • Check whether the organizational unit 1010 is properly mapped to the business partner within the system.

  3. Carrier Data in Transportation Management:

    • In some cases, you might need to check that the carrier master data is properly set up in TM, ensuring all necessary details are filled, such as business partner information, transportation zones, etc.

  4. Re-check Freight Agreement Setup:

    • Go back to the freight agreement setup process and verify if all the required fields are correctly populated, especially the business partner/carrier selection.

entry.title: Creating a commercial invoice for an overseas delivery
entry.content: I can only find pro-forma as an option, the carrier won't accept this, and I cannot remember how to create a commercial invoice from a sales order or even a delivery note.  The drop-down menu, within VF01, doesn't have an option close to what I need.  Is anyone able to assist please?

entry.title: Re: Rotational Weekend
entry.content:
Periodic Work Schedules have to be ceated and then Generated, and because you need Week 1 and Week 2 altenate for different groups of employees you will need 2 Periodic Work Schedule.

  Weekly Schedule 1 = Sat. Sun. Mon. Tues. Wedn. Thurs.  and Week Schedule 2 = OFF Sun. Mon. Tues. Wedn. Thurs.

Next you will need to create 2 Periodic Work Schedules, both starting on the same date but the one for the "Pair" week starting with Weekly Schedule 1 followed by Weekly Schedule 2, and the second one with Weekly Schedule 2 followed by Weekly Schedule 1.

Sincerely,

entry.title: Building Blocks of Innovation: Creating CAP Applications on SAP BTP
entry.content:
This blog aims to provide guidance for all ABAP developers on creating a CAP application, along with a fundamental understanding of SAP BTP.

 BTP:

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is a cloud-based platform designed to improve SAP applications. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) brings together application development and automation, data and analytics, integration, and AI capabilities in one unified environment.

 

 

SAP BTP Environments: 

  • In SAP BTP, “Environments” are like Platform-as-a-Service spaces.

 

  • They help us build, run, and manage applications and services.
  •  Think of BTP environments as different playgrounds, each made for specific tasks and technologies, providing the right space for different types of software work.

Each environment in SAP BTP provides the specific tools, technologies, and runtimes needed to build applications.

  • SAP BTP offers three main environments: Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma. There’s also another environment called Neo, but it’s being phased out.
  • In SAP BTP, an “environment” is like a digital workspace where you can create and run different types of software applications.

Think of it as having different rooms in a house for various tasks:

  • One room for building web apps (like Cloud Foundry)
  • Another room for working closely with SAP systems (like ABAP)
  • And another room for creating small, interconnected software pieces (like Kyma).
    ( Kyma is a cloud-based platform that combines Kubernetes with tools and open-source components to help develop, run, and manage secure, scalable cloud-native applications. )

Each environment is designed with the right tools and resources for different kinds of software projects, so businesses can easily pick the best fit for their needs within SAP BTP.

Cloud Foundry Environment (2017):

  • Think of this as a space for building modern, cloud-based applications. It’s like a workshop where you can create web and mobile apps using popular programming languages and tools. This environment is perfect for developers who want to build flexible and scalable software that runs on the internet.
  • Cloud Foundry includes the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime service, which is based on an open-source platform managed by the Cloud Foundry Foundation. It allows you to build new business apps and services, supporting multiple programming languages, libraries, and services.

Example use-case:

You could use the Cloud Foundry environment to build a web app with Node.js or the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. The app could connect to an SAP HANA database to pull and display real-time sales data.

ABAP Environment (2018):

  • SAP added the ABAP Environment to SAP BTP, known as SAP BTP ABAP Environment or “Steampunk.”
  • This environment is based on the latest version of the ABAP platform used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • It supports the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model, which includes SAP Fiori and Core Data Services (CDS).
  • The ABAP environment can also connect with other SAP BTP services, like SAP Destination service, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Workflow Management, and SAP Interactive Forms by Adobe.

Example use-case:

You can use the ABAP environment to create custom reports or build extensions for SAP S/4HANA, such as a specialized app for processing sales orders.

Kyma Environment (2020):

  • The Kyma environment is a fully managed Kubernetes runtime based on the open-source project “Kyma.” Kubernetes is the core technology behind Kyma, and Kyma adds extra features to make it more powerful.
  • Kyma combines Kubernetes with a set of top-notch tools and open-source projects, helping developers build, run, and manage secure and scalable cloud applications.
  • Kyma offers additional features like Serverless, Eventing, Observability, Security, Service Mesh, and Application Connectivity.
  •  It can integrate with other SAP services and products, like SAP BTP, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP SuccessFactors.
  • With Kyma, there’s no need to worry about infrastructure management or maintenance, freeing up developers to focus on building applications.

Neo Environment – Phasing Out in 2028:
·       The Neo environment has been around since 2012 and is the oldest SAP BTP environment.
·        However, it no longer offers the best technology for building cloud solutions. Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma now cover all the use cases Neo once did.
·        SAP has announced that the Neo environment will be phased out on December 31, 2028, depending on customer or partner contracts.

History of SAP BTP:

 

2012 - SAP entered the PaaS market with SAP NetWeaver Cloud, initially code-named “Neo” (short for NetWeaver On-Demand).

2013 - SAP NetWeaver Cloud was rebranded as SAP HANA Cloud Platform.

2017 - The Cloud Foundry environment was integrated alongside Neo.

2017 - The “HANA” branding was dropped, and the platform was renamed SAP Cloud Platform.

2018 - The ABAP environment was introduced within SAP Cloud Platform.

2020 - The Kyma environment was added to SAP Cloud Platform.

2021 - SAP Cloud Platform was retired, and the new brand, SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), was launched.

Introduction to Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP):

The SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is a set of tools and resources that helps developers create strong business applications. It shows them the best ways to do things, making it easier to follow proven methods. This means they spend less time on repetitive tasks and can focus more on solving their specific problems. The model uses popular open-source tools.

As previously mentioned, the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model includes various tools, languages, and libraries that assist in creating high-quality business services and applications. Here are some key components:

  • Business Application Studio
  • Core Data Services (CDS)
  • REST OData
  • SAP Fiori
  • SAP HANA
  • Java
  • Node.js
  • Eclipse VS Code CLI

In the realm of design principles, there exists a concept that is both flexible and subjective. It is flexible as it allows for a variety of choices. You have the freedom to select the technology you wish to employ, determine your architectural pattern, and choose the components of the programming model that align best with your development requirements.

Front Ends:

  • SAP Fiori
  • Angular
  • React
  • Vue

Toolsets:

  • SAP Business Application Studio
  • Eclipse
  • VS Code

Data Sources:

  • SAP HANA
  • SQLite

Environments:

  • Cloud Foundry
  • Kubernetes
  • XSA
  • Local

Note: Before creating application we need to install package for CDS in BAS

Steps to create Cap Application:

  • Create a account in BTP Cockpit
  • Click on Trail Home

 

  • Click SAP Business Application Studio

 

  • Open BAS Application
  • Login to BTP Account
  • Click on Dev i.e., Development Space

 

  • Click on menu and select Terminal and open new terminal

 

  • Type command cds init Employee_app

 

  • Now employee app is successfully created

 

  • Create a file in DB folder data-model.cds  

 

  • Should give unique name ex: cap_app1
  • Creating a entity called Employee

 

  • Now we need to expose the created data-model using service
  • Create a file in SRV cat-service.cds

 

  • We are now creating OData service

 

  • Now we need to install node module npm install

 

  • Build the cds using cds build

 

  • To run the application use command cds watch

 

 

  • Now you can check the metadata

 

 

  • In DB folder create a folder “data”

 

  • Create a CSV file for the data to the application
  • CSV file name should be according to namespace + Entity

     

cap_app1-Employee.csv

 

 

 

Please feel free to share your thoughts in the comments section below.

If you enjoyed reading this blog post about Clean ABAP and would like to receive similar content in the future, please consider following my profile. By following my profile gaddamarunkumar , you will receive updates on new blog posts and other content related to ABAP development best practices.

Follow the SAP ABAP environment Topic page: SAP ABAP

This page is a great resource for staying up-to-date on the latest news and developments in the SAP HANA environment. You can follow the page to receive updates, post and answer questions, and read other posts on the topic

If you have any queries related to SAP and ABAP Developments you can post here https://answers.sap.com/tags/73554900100700000996, you can also find many topics related to your query.

entry.title: Trigger event from SAP Document Management Service
entry.content: Is it possible to receive a notification / event / or call webhook when a file is uploaded / changed in SAP Document Management Service.

entry.title: Re: Professional Super User Not Able To Cancel Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi,

I hope you have checked the General Authorizations and Additional Authorization Creator.

You can also check by creating a new user and assigning it the Profession User License and check if it works in that.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Build Process Automation or Document Information Extraction, Do we have a tool to verify digital signatures?

If not does anybody know on an external service that I can use for this purpose from SAP Build Process Automation?

Thanks

SAP Build Process Automation Document Information Extraction

entry.title: Re: Difference in AP Report and Ledger Reconciliation
entry.content:
Hi,

Without screenshot of the selection criteria of both the reports, the actual situation cannot be determined. Are you running the Aging Report on a backdate? If yes, have you checked the 'Display Vendors with Zero Balance'.

Share or check the selection criteria as a basic check.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Application is crashing after some time while exporti...

entry.title: Certify Partner application built with SAP BTP and integrating/extending with SAP Business One.
entry.content:
SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) is happy to announce the availability for our partners to certify SAP Business One extensions built with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). This ensures the extensions are built for the latest innovative platform, SAP BTP, and are cloud-ready and future-proof.

In particular partners catering to customers in the SME segment, who are considering a journey to GROW with SAP and S/4HANA Cloud public edition, will appreciate that SAP BTP allows them to build their extensions on a central platform for both solutions.

In 2024, SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) aligned the certification portfolio to best suit the needs of our innovation partners to further certify their apps, extensions, and integrations. We are looking forward to supporting our partners’ innovation journey and publishing many more SAP-certified solutions integrated with our strategic technology and application portfolio.

SAP ICC phased out certification services for certain integration certification scenarios including SAP Business One extensions (B1-SDK, B1-HANA, B1-CLOUD). To support partners and customers who value an “SAP-certified” designation for their partner add-ons and extensions, we now offer a certification scenario specifically catering to SAP Business One extensions built with SAP BTP.

New SAP Business One Premier Partner designation: (Update on PE program changes)

With an ecosystem of 80,000 customers and 800 active partners supporting the small enterprise segment, we believe it's important for partners focused on SAP Business One to remain competitive while transitioning (if not already) to the SAP cloud portfolio.

The new designation "SAP Business One Premier Partner" will allow our top performing partners to differentiate themselves and keep excelling in the small enterprise market space.

While we encourage all our current partners with a focus on SAP Business One to strive for the new designation, we equally encourage them to embrace the SAP cloud portfolio and endeavor to achieve the SAP Silver Partner or SAP Gold Partner level.

Detailed information: PE program changes

About SAP BTP:

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) brings together data and analytics, artificial intelligence, application development, automation, and integration in one, unified environment. SAP BTP is the agile platform for digital transformation, with comprehensive application development services and capabilities that allows businesses to collect, manage, analyze, and leverage information of all types, to extend and connect to business systems, and to innovate new edge scenarios to allow the business to continuously adapt and advance. 

It enables customers to achieve business agility, create a truly integrated and optimized enterprise, and accelerate digital transformation across the business – all without the requirement of maintaining or investing in on-premises infrastructure. The multi-cloud foundation of SAP BTP supports multiple environments such as Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Kyma, as well as multiple different regions, and a broad choice of programming languages.

BTP is also the foundation of the clean core concept. It enables companies to extend and integrate without changing the core of the application but still allows to extend and customize business processes to their very specific needs.

List of SAP BTP Services: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog

 

Technical Approaches for SAP Business One Extensibility via SAP BTP:

  • Partner application deployed on SAP BTP and integrating with SAP Business One via SAP Cloud connectors using SAP Business One service layer.

Link to implement it: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/connecting-sap-btp-and-on-premise-sap-business-one-using-the-sap-cloud/ba-p/13931142

Service Layer API reference link: https://help.sap.com/doc/056f69366b5345a386bb8149f1700c19/10.0/en-US/Service%20Layer%20API%20Reference.html

 

  • Partner application deployed on SAP BTP and developing extensions for SAP Business One, web client.

Developing Extensions for SAP Business One Link: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-extensions-for-sap-business-one-and-the-web-client

Exploring SAP Business One, web client: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-sap-business-one-web-client

Best Practices for Technical Implementation of SAP Business One Web Client link: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/best-practices-for-technical-implementation-of-sap-business-one-web-client/ba-p/13801658

 

  • Partner application deployed on SAP BTP and developing extensions for SAP Business One.

In this case, deployment on SAP BTP as well as add-on deployment on SAP Business One will be checked as a part of BTP-EXT-B1 Certification.

 

Technical Approaches for SAP Business One Integration via SAP BTP

If the partner application is deployed on platforms (other than SAP BTP), it can utilize SAP BTP’s Integration Suite service to build integration content to integrate with SAP Business One. This qualifies for IS-PI-CNT certification.

If you develop a content package on SAP Integration Suite (within Cloud Integration capability), then please contact your ICC technical consultant to get appropriate certification documentation.

 

Support for SAP Business One Extensibility, community page:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/business-one-extensibility

https://community.sap.com/t5/c-khhcw49343/SAP+Business+One+Extensibility/pd-p/37d7341b-a1cf-461e-9e60-47ede025c729

 

For partners who want to integrate with or extend SAP Business One – also in view of future planned integrations with S/4HANA Cloud public edition - our recommended approach is to build extensions and integrations with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Partners can apply for this new certification scenario (called BTP-EXT-B1) via Partner Benefit Catalog – PBC.

 

To understand the complete certification requirements and pre-requisites of BTP-EXT-B1 certification, please reach out to icc@sap.com for further assistance.

entry.title: S4HANA Cloud Create Outgoing Invoices (FB70) - how to get notes (item long text)?
entry.content:
Hi,

I've been trying to get notes (item long text) entered using GUI app Create Outgoing Invoices (FB70) in S4 Cloud.

And I've seen this KBA 2971277 - Long text is not displayed in Manage Journal Entries.

But it doesn't say how I get the notes that I have entered. I want to find out if there is any CDS view that can support this.

Has anyone been able to get Item long text in FB70 in S4 Cloud?

Cheers,

Tri

entry.title: Re: Bank Reconciliation Accounts in S/4HANA 2020
entry.content: Very beautifully explained...

entry.title: About the LMS Environment
entry.content:
グループ会社のインスタンスに対してインスタンスリフレッシュを行い、EC環境を作成しました。
LMSについては、プロビジョニングを通じて環境(シェル)を作成し、それをSAPにECとの連携を依頼しました。

現在は、グループ会社の構成設定をLMS環境に反映させたいと考えています。
別のインスタンスの更新を実行する必要がある場合はお知らせください。
また、具体的な手順をご提案いただけると幸いです。

ご支援のほどよろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Re: Use of elements not permitted in CDS view Data Defini...
entry.content:
Hi kkh0125.

The reason I ran into this error is because I was using component ZLOCAL to create my package and CDS view. In this component you can only use ABAP Language Version "Abap for Cloud Development", and there are restrictions in this ABAP language. ABAP Cloud does not allow you to use all objects as in on-premise development, hence the error.

You need to use the non Cloud ABAP language so make sure to create your package in a software component that allows you to do so. Once you have selected the correct ABAP language version the error will disappear. 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: QM Catalog code group/code catalog does not support '...
entry.content:
Greetings,

I would assume that SAP made a restriction. I would suggest manual migration.

It might be connected to the following error message. This error message is displayed if you want to maintain catalogue 3 in QS41.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Use of elements not permitted in CDS view Data Defini...

entry.title: Re: QTD functionality on Group Financial Statements Revie...
entry.content:
In Group Financial Statements APP. if you choose Quarter, system only retrieve data based on quarter.

You cannot retrieve January + February data. System must retrieve full quarter data and ignore your input period data in filter.

For your demand, I guess you need to create a custom report in Group data analysis.

entry.content:
Hi, 

Can anyone please help to explain if we can plan currency rates for future time periods in SAC like BPC?

If so, can you please explain the process for the same.

Regards,

Lakshmi

@N1kh1l

entry.title: Re: Charts in Report stories are not getting refreshed wi...
entry.content: They havent come back on this. Waiting for their reply. Thank you for taking your time in understanding my problem statement and helping me.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic restricted key figure in analytic model
entry.content:
Hello @shlomi_weiss 

The problem as of now is, that there are no customer exit variables to define your own coding.

So you can only use a trick and generate custom columns in the Fact View, which return the required information for correct key figure restriction...

E.g. for Current Year and last year you can use: 

CASE LEFT(InvoiceDate,4)
WHEN LEFT(NOW(),4)  THEN 'CurrentYear'
WHEN LEFT(NOW(),4)-1 THEN 'LastYear'
ELSE ''
END

Similar for last month:

CASE
WHEN Date > ADD_MONTHS_LAST(LAST_DAY(CURRENT_DATE),-1) and Date <= ADD_MONTHS_LAST(LAST_DAY(CURRENT_DATE),0) THEN 'Current Month'
WHEN Date > ADD_MONTHS_LAST(LAST_DAY(CURRENT_DATE),-2) and Date <= ADD_MONTHS_LAST(LAST_DAY(CURRENT_DATE),-1) THEN 'Last Month'
END

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩

entry.title: error when trying to connect to public cloud connection from eclipse
entry.content:
Hi,

 I am trying to connect to SAP Public Cloud system from eclipse via the connection url provided by the client which looks like this. I am using eclipse 2024-12(latest) version and it is showing "You have been successfully logged on

You can close this page and continue in the ABAP Development Tools." in the browser but in eclipse it is showing "Logon to the service instance has failed." Kindly suggest if any solution is there.

entry.title: Amount got changed in accounting document after invoice released to accounting
entry.content:
Hi All,
we are using F&A pricing , There are two invoices :
invoice 1: one invoice having pricing based on bill of lading(Goods Issue).

Here Goods Issue date is 28-02-2024, and the invoice contains the billing date as 28-02-204, in the sales order billing date is 27-02-2024. Even though the system considered a bill of lading pricing still after the invoice was released to accounting the amount was changed in the accounting document. the difference amount in the accounting document is  9 rs

invoice 2: one invoice has pricing based on the pricing date. 

The pricing date is 18-01-2024, but invoiced on 19-01-2024 which is considered as a bill of lading pricing (Goods Issue) why because the system since the bill of lading happened on 19-01-2024, so when they released to accounting the amount got changed in the accounting document. the difference amount in the accounting document is 40K rs.

please provide your valuable suggestions @Sapsd  @sapoil&gas.

#sapsd #sapoil&gas

entry.title: Re: Performance Optimization in field symbols
entry.content:
I think you actually don't need the assign component statement.

<fs_data>-fpm_bol_guibb_ref_0000000009 = abap_true 

should do the job.

Apart from that I don't think there should be a performance issue at all. Maybe check with transaction SAT and check the hit list, concentrate on the statements with most net time.

entry.title: Re: Organizational Unit error for business partner
entry.content:
Hi, thanks for helping out with troubleshooting. Here are my responses.

#1, #3: Yes, I have extended the BP to the roles carrier, supplier and supplier (Fin.Accounting). In a private cloud, I would be checking the location to ensure the the carrier is available but I do not have access to do that in the public cloud system.

#2: Not sure how I can check this as we have limited access in the public cloud system.

#4: I have rechecked a few times so am not sure whether there is any issue with the FA set up. I was able to assign a different carrier and was allowed to save. At the moment, I have saved with the other carrier just so that I do not lose the FA data I set up.

as I already mentioned, the password for system user and abap schema user in hana is the same as for user developer in netweaver (in case of ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022, SP01  it is ABAPtr2022#01 at the moment of writing )

entry.title: Re: Make invisible Purchasing Group in ME51N
entry.content: What do you mean by "Purchase Group 120 has left from the BUYERS ."? Is it possible that this group is no longer in use? Can you simply reassign existing documents to a different Pr grp and then delete 120 from the system? Alternatively you can rename it to Do not use and remove from the authorization object.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori: Searching by App-ID
entry.content:
While its not possible to search an App by its ID directly in Fiori Launchpad there is way around via the SAP-GUI: 
In GUI you can simply enter the App-ID in the transaction search bar and you will be redirected to the launchpad in your browser 👍

entry.title: Re: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello,

I am also facing the same error. Any resolution steps?

entry.title: Re: multiple brands for one material in SAP MM
entry.content:
Hello @2831990 

You really need to provide more details about your brand management process, e.g.:

  • How to you plan and execute brands sourcing? Do you purchase or produce them? Do you keep them on stock or produce MTO or maybe you just brand a generic product just before shipment?
  • How do you value brands stock? Are they all valued at the same price, how big are price differences?
  • What is your pricing process?
  • What do you do if a customer orders brand A, which you don't have on stock? Do you produce it MTO, or do you offer a different brand?

As you see the questions are numerous and they all impact how brands can be modeled and implemented.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Hi, I am also facing exactly same error.

Conversion of customer-defined Codepages - report RSCP0126
We have code page 9.. for the printer , in this report RSCP0126 have a field code page to converted
1. do we keep code page blank as in the above link
2.or to specify the code page 9XX to ...

Thank you

JSP

entry.title: Re: BPC and Group Reporting
entry.content:
nice overview.

A couple of comments on GR (Group Reporting:

- source: we currently also support sources: ECC and oData, and plan to explore BDC datasphere and data products as sources as well

- group structure: Planned innovation on roadmap for parent relationship for groups

entry.title: Re: Make invisible Purchasing Group in ME51N
entry.content:
@TobiaszWegrzynski 

"Purchase Group 120 has left from the BUYERS ." most probably means that a person responsible for purchasing group 120 has chosen to pursue further career goals outside of the organization.

I would not recommend reassigning existing documents to another purchasing group. Purchasing reporting would go haywire if you do that.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP Business Unleashed: A Look into the Future
entry.content:
Digitalization is changing our working environment faster than ever before. With SAP Business Unleashed, SAP has provided an exciting outlook on the future of its business software – with many new opportunities for companies to optimize their processes and make them more efficient.

The Way Forward: Data, AI and Integration

SAP pursues a clear strategy: use data intelligently, use artificial intelligence (AI sensibly, and connect different systems seamlessly. A central component of this is the SAP Business Data Cloud , which helps companies link internal and external data sources and thus make better decisions.

Particularly exciting is Joule , an AI-powered copilot that is designed to automate business processes and support teams in their daily work. Joule promises to not only take on repetitive tasks, but also provide valuable insights that make decisions easier.

What makes the new SAP strategy strong?

1. Real-time data and analytics Thanks to SAP Business Data Cloud, companies can not only collect data, but also analyze it in real time. This means faster reactions to market changes and more informed decisions.

2. Artificial intelligence as a game changer With Joule, SAP is launching an AI solution that can do much more than simple automation. It understands processes, analyzes data and supports teams in their daily work - a real competitive advantage.

3. Openness and integration SAP no longer only relies on its own solutions, but is increasingly integrating with other platforms - for example through its partnership with Databricks . This gives companies more flexibility to further develop their existing systems instead of replacing them completely.

4. Real-world examples Henkel, Bosch and other companies have shown how they use SAP technologies to optimize their data strategy and thus work more efficiently. These examples make it clear that SAP is not just talking about the future, but is already putting it into practice.

 

Still open questions: What remains unclear?

Despite the promising developments, there are some aspects that should be examined in more detail:

1. How is the implementation carried out?

  • What concrete steps do companies need to take to integrate the SAP Business Data Cloud into their existing IT landscape?
  • What challenges might arise during implementation and how can they be solved?

2. Data protection and security

  • What measures has SAP taken to comply with data protection guidelines and minimize security risks?
  • How is sensitive company data protected from unauthorized access?

3. Costs and ROI

  • What investments are required to benefit from the new SAP technologies?
  • Are there already concrete figures or experience reports that illustrate the return on investment (ROI)?

Conclusion: Set the course for tomorrow now

With SAP Business Unleashed , it is clear that SAP wants to help companies work even more intelligently and in a more connected way. AI, data integration and flexible platforms are no longer a thing of the future - they are now available. Companies that rely on them early on will secure clear competitive advantages.

entry.title: Re: Prompted for username and password when running Fiori...
entry.content:
Hello Sulman,

please make sure to follow the steps under 1. Generating and Deploying Your Application in Develop an SAP Fiori Application UI and Deploy it to ABAP Using Visual Studio Code. It also includes steps to preview the Fiori application.

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: Make invisible Purchasing Group in ME51N
entry.content: @DominikTylczyn your point is valid, but we know too little to be sure, we dont know what the reason for that change is, what is the volume of documents, etc.

entry.title: Requirement Gathering Questionnare for SAP SuccessFactors SP
entry.content:
Dear all,

Do you have list of questions that should be asked by SAP SuccessFactors Consultants to implement Succession Planning? Please list here asap.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Server Migration of SAP BI 4.3 SP4 Windows server 201...
entry.content:
Hi sgore,

I faced a similar scenario in the past. As a mandatory step you need to do a fresh SAP BO 4.3 installation on W22 servers (be sure that you are installing "exactly" the same release-patch you installed on the W19 servers).

At that point you have to options to move the full repository content from W19 to W22 SAP BO install

1) To perform a full system promotion as described below using Command Line Interface (CLI)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM/2e167338c1b24da9b2a94e68efd79c42/033c81d383af4947ac8d56e68cde1522.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=4.3.4

This is a well know approach and you will find plenty of references about it including some interesting guides such this one

https://help.sap.com/doc/pattern-book-on-life-cycle-managementpromotion-management/4.2/en-US/8%20Pattern%20Book%20on%20Life%20Cycle%20Management-%20Promotion%20Management.pdf

2) Create a cluster. This is a very clever approach. Consists on create a cluster of CMS. When doing it all the content is "automatically" synchronized across both set of servers. Once systems are aligned, remove W19 servers from the cluster and shutdown them.

This second approach is poor documented and it´s a bit messy but provides an straight forward path if you are able to complete it successfully (it's my favourite one)

General recommendations (for whatever approach you use): Take backups of servers and test all process steps in advance on a test environment.

Regards,

Alfons

entry.title: Query regarding BTP degradation activities
entry.content:
Hello All,

All our subaccounts are in Data center Frankfurt or ROT. I want know whether Degradation activity in any other Data Center ..let's say Walldorf affects our subaccounts or services?

If we expand the event list for Data center Walldorf, the system ID which is shown is same system id as our subaccounts which are mapped to Data center Rot or Frankfurt.

Because of this, we are not sure if activity in Walldorf affects the Rot or Franfurt specially when same system ID shows under events.

Thanks in Advance

Amar Sinha

entry.title: Re: FIORI Elements Field Highlighting not working in CREATE Mode but EDIT Mode
entry.content: Hi , its a V2 Service

entry.title: SAP Business Unleashed: A Look into the Future
entry.content:
Digitalization is changing our working environment faster than ever before. With SAP Business Unleashed, SAP has provided an exciting outlook on the future of its business software – with many new opportunities for companies to optimize their processes and make them more efficient.

The Way Forward: Data, AI and Integration

SAP pursues a clear strategy: use data intelligently, use artificial intelligence (AI sensibly, and connect different systems seamlessly. A central component of this is the SAP Business Data Cloud , which helps companies link internal and external data sources and thus make better decisions.

Particularly exciting is Joule , an AI-powered copilot that is designed to automate business processes and support teams in their daily work. Joule promises to not only take on repetitive tasks, but also provide valuable insights that make decisions easier.

What makes the new SAP strategy strong?

1. Real-time data and analytics Thanks to SAP Business Data Cloud, companies can not only collect data, but also analyze it in real time. This means faster reactions to market changes and more informed decisions.

2. Artificial intelligence as a game changer With Joule, SAP is launching an AI solution that can do much more than simple automation. It understands processes, analyzes data and supports teams in their daily work - a real competitive advantage.

3. Openness and integration SAP no longer only relies on its own solutions, but is increasingly integrating with other platforms - for example through its partnership with Databricks . This gives companies more flexibility to further develop their existing systems instead of replacing them completely.

4. Real-world examples Henkel, Bosch and other companies have shown how they use SAP technologies to optimize their data strategy and thus work more efficiently. These examples make it clear that SAP is not just talking about the future, but is already putting it into practice.

 

Still open questions: What remains unclear?

Despite the promising developments, there are some aspects that should be examined in more detail:

1. How is the implementation carried out?

  • What concrete steps do companies need to take to integrate the SAP Business Data Cloud into their existing IT landscape?
  • What challenges might arise during implementation and how can they be solved?

2. Data protection and security

  • What measures has SAP taken to comply with data protection guidelines and minimize security risks?
  • How is sensitive company data protected from unauthorized access?

3. Costs and ROI

  • What investments are required to benefit from the new SAP technologies?
  • Are there already concrete figures or experience reports that illustrate the return on investment (ROI)?

Conclusion: Set the course for tomorrow now

With SAP Business Unleashed , it is clear that SAP wants to help companies work even more intelligently and in a more connected way. AI, data integration and flexible platforms are no longer a thing of the future - they are now available. Companies that rely on them early on will secure clear competitive advantages.

The most important question is no longer whether you should use AI and data analysis – but how quickly you should start.

entry.title: Re: Can we plan currency rates in sap analytics planning
entry.content:
@pragnya_m 

You can maintain rates in FX tables for future periods. I am not sure what exactly do you mean by plan future Fx rates. Plan/Budget data is generally future looking so yes you can maintain FX rates for future periods and run FX translation for future periods.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Rabax during SAPGUI logon - While Basis support package apply???
entry.content: This SAP Note is NOT present now. Any alternative SAP Note present..? Thanks

entry.title: Re: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content: @PL1 Thank you for your reply. Is this clarification documented somewhere public? How would a customer know the details of the timeline as the help portal description does not specify which module is subjected to the maintenance date?

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud: Unifying Enterprise Data for Next-Generation Insights
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP’s Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a new cloud-based offering designed to unify data from various SAP applications and beyond, giving business users a single, consistent view of information. With BDC, enterprises can integrate and analyze data from multiple sources more efficiently, eliminating the hassle of juggling disconnected tools. The result: faster, more reliable insights to make better business decisions.

Why Business Data Cloud?

  1. Simplicity: Instead of manually stitching together data from different systems, BDC automates data integration using a concept called “Data Products.”
  2. Out-of-the-Box Insights: BDC provides pre-built “Insight Apps,” which let you see and explore your data right away.
  3. Openness: BDC can bring in data from both SAP and non-SAP sources. You get a more comprehensive picture of your organization’s data without dealing with multiple, siloed solutions.

Key Concepts

1. Data Products

A “Data Product” is a curated dataset drawn from SAP or other systems, ready for immediate use. These datasets contain everything you need for analytics or AI tasks—no extra wrangling required. They’re designed with standardized structures so they can be easily reused and shared.

2. BDC Foundation Services

These behind-the-scenes services handle the heavy lifting:

  • Data Extraction: Pulling information from source systems like SAP S/4HANA.
  • Transformation: Cleaning and structuring the data so it’s analytics-ready.
  • Publishing: Making Data Products available for consumption via modern protocols like Delta Sharing.

Foundation Services are internal to SAP, so you won’t see them in the user interface. Instead, they make sure your Data Products are always up-to-date and accessible.

3. SAP BDC (The Customer-Facing Part)

SAP BDC is the “front door” that customers actually use. It includes:

  • BDC Cockpit: A single dashboard for administrators to configure data pipelines, onboard data sources, and manage user access.
  • Analytics & Visualization: Built on SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, so you can build dashboards, run analytics, and even do planning—without juggling separate tools.
  • Insight Apps: Pre-built templates that deliver out-of-the-box reports and analytics. For instance, you might get a ready-made “Procurement Insight App” for spend analysis.

4. Integration with Existing SAP Solutions

BDC leverages existing technologies like SAP HANA Cloud and Spark, plus new features such as HANA Data Lake Files (HDLF), to manage and store data. It also integrates with Databricks (optional) for advanced data engineering and machine learning scenarios.

What Problems Does BDC Solve?

  • Data Silos: BDC pulls data from across your organization into one place, cutting down on duplication and conflicting data definitions.
  • Complex Setups: With a single cockpit, you can set up data connections and maintain them centrally, instead of dealing with multiple configuration screens in different tools.
  • Slow Insights: By standardizing how data is published, you can quickly spin up analytics or AI projects—spending more time on innovation and less time on setup.

Looking Ahead

BDC represents SAP’s ongoing evolution to meet modern data needs. In the future, expect even deeper integration with AI and machine learning platforms, broader support for non-SAP data sources, and expanded partner solutions.

Getting Started

  1. Identify Data Sources: Decide which SAP or third-party systems contain the information you need.
  2. Check Available Insight Apps: Explore pre-built analytics templates that might already deliver the insights you want.
  3. Use BDC Cockpit: Manage data onboarding, security, and user access—all in one place.

Conclusion

SAP Business Data Cloud aims to simplify data integration and empower both technical and non-technical users to tap into real-time, trusted data. With pre-built Data Products, a robust foundation for data sharing, and user-friendly analytics tools, BDC helps organizations quickly uncover insights and make smarter decisions.

For more detailed technical information, refer to SAP’s internal architecture documents on BDC.

entry.title: S4 HAA Public cloud to GTS system connection- SM59
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

I am from SAP GTS functional consultant, we are struggling with RFC connection issue from S4 HANA public cloud to GTS system connection.

Allready, we have raised to SAP reg this issue and they said it would be with Networking or Firewall team, in the meantime I am asking our basis team to cross check in SM59 whether it is correct feeder system RFC is maintained but they suugested ask SAP as we dont have much knowledge when they are connecting S/4 HANA public cloud to GTS connection.

Would you please check our GTS -DGT system and let us know.

System connections are allready opened. let us know if you need any inputs.

SAP Reference ticket - 1373308/2024

entry.title: Re: Delete a row based on a condition in Adobe form
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you try using "Hidden" feature for all the individual Line Items Text Elements OR for the whole Line Item Row, 

Something like,

if (wbs_text.rawValue = Null)  { 
wbs_text.presence = "hidden";  
}

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: multiple brands for one material in SAP MM
entry.content:
The best approach depends on business requirements, but using Split Valuation
is generally the preferred solution in SAP MM. This allows a single material code to
represent multiple brands while distinguishing them through valuation types. Each brand can have its own
valuation type, enabling separate stock tracking, pricing, and valuation without creating duplicate material codes.
This approach reduces master data maintenance and simplifies procurement and inventory management. However, if different brands require distinct attributes,
BOMs, or procurement processes, separate material codes might be justified. Overall, Split Valuation by Brand
ensures better efficiency and streamlined data management.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure "standard text key" for task list &...
entry.content:
To configure the Standard Text Key for an Equipment Task List (IA01) and Maintenance Order (IW31), follow these steps:
1. Define Standard Text Key: Go to SPRO → Plant Maintenance and Customer Service → Maintenance Plans, Work Centers, Task Lists → Task Lists → Define Standard Text Key and create a new key.
2. Assign Standard Text Key to Task List: In IA01, enter the Standard Text Key in the operation details under the External Processing tab.
3. Use in Maintenance Order: In IW31, when selecting a task list, the Standard Text Key will be copied into the order operation.
4. Maintain Standard Texts: Use transaction SO10 to create predefined text entries that can be linked to the Standard Text Key.
5. Test and Validate: Create a task list and a maintenance order to ensure the text key functions correctly.

entry.title: Post Payment - Emailing Payment Advices
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, do we have the feature to notify via email to suppliers/vendors once the payment is made?

If yes, please advise steps to configure the same in SAP Public Cloud 2502 version.

Thanks much.

Best regards,
Krishna

entry.title: Futuristic Proposals: Elevating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
As enterprises continue to demand real‑time insights and seamless data integration, SAP Sales Cloud V2 is evolving to meet these challenges. Looking forward, integrating the powerful capabilities of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) can drive transformative change—making Sales Cloud V2 not only smarter but also more agile and secure.

1. Real‑Time Data Products for Instant Insights

BDC’s data products are pre‑curated, governed datasets that capture critical business information in real time. By integrating these with SAP Sales Cloud V2, sales teams can access:

  • Live Customer Data: Instantly view the most current customer interactions and behaviors.
  • Dynamic Pipeline Visibility: Leverage continuously updated sales opportunity data to drive faster decision‑making.

Such real‑time insights help sales teams anticipate customer needs and react swiftly to market changes, ensuring that decisions are always data‑driven.

2. Enhanced AI and Predictive Analytics

BDC’s integration with cutting‑edge AI platforms, including Databricks, enables the creation of advanced insight apps that run predictive models on unified data sets. For example:

  • Predictive Sales Forecasting: AI‑driven models can analyze historical and current sales data to forecast trends and identify high‑value opportunities.
  • Next‑Best Action Recommendations: Sales representatives receive real‑time suggestions based on customer behavior and market dynamics.

These capabilities are already being showcased in recent SAP announcements, where AI insights are turbocharging business decision‑making citeturn0search15.

3. Unified Customer Data Ecosystem

Integrating BDC with Sales Cloud V2 provides a unified, single‑version-of‑the‑truth for customer data:

  • Consistent Data Governance: With BDC’s robust versioning and data governance, sales data remains accurate and secure across multiple systems.
  • Seamless Integration Across SAP Solutions: Whether it’s data from SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, or third‑party sources, BDC harmonizes disparate data into one accessible ecosystem citeturn0search8.

This unified approach reduces data silos and ensures that sales strategies are built on reliable, high‑quality data.

4. Customizable and Adaptive Insight Apps

BDC’s framework supports the development of both standard and custom insight apps:

  • Tailored Sales Dashboards: Pre‑built templates and customizable apps enable sales managers to monitor KPIs, pipeline health, and performance metrics.
  • Low-Code/No-Code Enhancements: Leveraging platforms like SAP Build, organizations can quickly adapt the solution to meet unique business requirements without heavy development overhead citeturn0search17.

Such flexibility ensures that as market needs evolve, your sales cloud remains agile and responsive.

5. Automated Data Governance and Security

As sales data becomes increasingly valuable, securing it is paramount. BDC’s advanced data governance features can:

  • Automate Compliance: Enforce data protection standards and automate data lifecycle management.
  • Ensure Data Integrity: Use semantic versioning and controlled updates to protect the integrity of sales data.

This foundation of trust and security is critical for organizations operating in regulated industries.

6. Future‑Proof Architecture

Integrating BDC with SAP Sales Cloud V2 offers a forward‑looking, microservices‑based architecture:

  • Scalability and Flexibility: The cloud‑native design allows the system to scale effortlessly, accommodating growing data volumes and evolving business needs.
  • Continuous Innovation: With BDC’s ability to rapidly deploy new AI models and insight apps, organizations can continually innovate without disruption.

This future‑proof architecture not only supports current requirements but also positions your sales cloud to adapt to emerging trends and technologies.

Conclusion

By harnessing the power of SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Sales Cloud V2 can be transformed into a truly best‑in‑class solution. Real‑time data products, AI‑driven insights, a unified customer data ecosystem, customizable apps, automated governance, and a future‑proof architecture collectively enable sales teams to work smarter, faster, and more securely. As organizations look to gain a competitive edge in an ever‑evolving market, integrating BDC with Sales Cloud V2 is a strategic move toward a data‑driven, agile future.

Embrace these futuristic proposals and unlock the full potential of your sales organization.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Cloud Create Outgoing Invoices (FB70) - how to...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud, the Item Long Text (Notes) entered in FB70 (Create Outgoing Invoices) is typically stored in the long text table (STXL)
and linked via object key references. However, standard CDS views may not always expose this directly.
To retrieve the Item Long Text, try using:
1. CDS View I_JournalEntryItemText – This might provide text details for journal entries.
2. Read Text using Read_Text Function Module (for on-premise, limited in Cloud).
3. Check Extensibility – Use Custom Fields and Logic (APP ID: F1481) to extend standard views.
4. Explore API: Journal Entry - Read (A2X) – This may provide text-related details.
